content.problemStatement:
While Alice was drinking sugarcane juice, she started wondering about the following facts:


The juicer sells each glass of sugarcane juice for 505050 coins.
He spends 20%20\%20% of his total income on buying sugarcane.
He spends 20%20\%20% of his total income on buying salt and mint leaves.
He spends 30%30\%30% of his total income on shop rent.
Alice wonders, what is the juicer's profit (in coins) when he sells NNN glasses of sugarcane juice?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT — the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN, as described in the problem statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the juicer's profit when he sells NNN glasses of juice.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the number of glasses sold.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the juicer's profit in coins.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each glass yields 50 coins. Profit = 15 * N. For N=2 profit=30, N=4 profit=60, N=5 profit=75, N=10 profit=150.

solution.approach: Each glass is sold for 50 coins. The total income for N glasses is 50 * N. The juicer spends 20% + 20% + 30% = 70% of the income, so the remaining profit is 30% of the income. Profit = 0.30 * 50 * N = 15 * N. For each test case we simply read N and output 15 * N.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
